content.full_text:
TO: ADVICE REQUEST MEETING PARTICIPANTS

TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY   ( ) DAN
[ ] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA   ( ) JOHN K
[ ] ROGER   [ ] CARLA   ( ) MARY ANN
[ ] HELEN   [ ] OTHER

FROM: J. Pritchard
DATE: 9/10/85

[XX] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 82015; 18215

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER Fred Lowell
REPRESENTING Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro
PHONE NUMBER:

QUESTION: A legislator is invited to a campaign event. The legislator arranges transportation to the event in a corporation's airplane. How is the transportation reported?

ANSWER: The transportation is reported as an in-kind contribution to the legislator. Previously, we have advised that when a candidate or committee who is holding an event arranges transportation for guests, the transportation is an in-kind contribution to the candidate or committee holding the event. This situation, however, is different because the transportation is not provided at the behest of the candidate or committee holding the event, but rather at the behest of the candidate attending the event.
